versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 18 So He will repay according to their deeds:
19 So shall they fear the name of the LORD
and His glory where it rises.
For He will come like a raging flood,
driven by the breath of the LORD.
to those in Jacob who turn from transgression,”

21 “As for Me, this is My covenant with them,” says the LORD. “My Spirit will not depart from you, and My words that I have put in your mouth will not depart from your mouth or from the mouths of your children and grandchildren, from now on and forevermore,” says the LORD.

context_lines: In **Isaiah 59**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Covenant of the Redeemer** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 59** remains the first control on meaning.
